_doc: Layer 2b (technical) explanation templates per (primary_tag, error_type, mt_system). Consulted by tompe.pipeline.explanation_generator.generate_layer2b_explanation; cache misses fall back to LLM generation. To curate: add entries to `entries[]` with non-empty `technical_description`, `key_concepts`, and `references` fields. `mt_system` may be omitted to match any system.

entries.technical_description: Cross-lingual lexical interference between orthographically aligned but semantically divergent word pairs. Subword tokenisation (BPE / SentencePiece) frequently yields shared subword units across the language pair, biasing the encoder–decoder attention toward the high-probability surface-form mapping. The phenomenon is amplified when training data contains parallel sentences with literal renderings.
references: Frunza & Inkpen (2009), Identification of disambiguation strategies for cognates and false friends; Carpuat & Wu (2007), Improving SMT performance through false-friend disambiguation.

entries.technical_description: Cross-lingual divergence in grammatical gender features. Where the source language under-specifies gender (English), the decoder must infer it from target-side monolingual priors and from the alignment of the target noun. Errors arise when (a) the noun is low-frequency in training data, (b) gender agreement crosses a long dependency, or (c) the antecedent is structurally distant.
references: Vanmassenhove et al. (2018), Getting gender right in NMT; Stanovsky et al. (2019), Evaluating gender bias in MT.
